agent.root_goal: Produce a bilingual (EN/DE) developer style guide for 'Halden', an internal smart-building automation platform, in two independently-owned parts. Part A (device standards team): first survey the naming actually used by Halden's three legacy controllers (hvac-controller, lighting-bridge, access-gateway), recording every legacy point name, telemetry channel, and alarm code in use; then decide and write the canonical terminology standard (point nouns, telemetry-channel vocabulary, alarm-code casing rules). The published standard lists ONLY the new canonical terms, not the legacy ones. Part B (integrator documentation team): write the worked integration tutorial with runnable configuration samples, covering four sections in both languages: the HVAC control flow, the lighting-scene flow, alarm handling, and migration notes for installers upgrading from the legacy controllers - the migration notes must map each legacy point name and alarm code to its canonical replacement, old -> new. Facilities handbook rule, applying to the whole guide: documentation ships only if every point name, telemetry channel, and alarm code appearing anywhere in the guide matches the published canonical standard exactly; term drift between parts is a shipping defect.

action.reasoning: The root task requires producing a bilingual developer style guide split into two parts. Part A (survey and canonical standard) must complete before Part B (tutorial and migration notes) can begin, because Part B depends on the canonical terms and the legacy-to-canonical mapping. I will spawn two main subtasks: root.1 for Part A and root.2 for Part B. root.2 will be gated on the completion of root.1. Each subtask will further decompose as needed. I will take a self_role to perform final integration and validation to ensure term consistency across both parts.

subtasks.goal: Survey legacy controllers and produce the canonical terminology standard for Halden.
subtasks.capsule: This subtask handles Part A. It must survey the three legacy controllers (hvac-controller, lighting-bridge, access-gateway) to record all legacy point names, telemetry channels, and alarm codes. Then it must define the canonical terminology standard (point nouns, telemetry-channel vocabulary, alarm-code casing rules). The published standard lists only new canonical terms. This output is a hard dependency for Part B's migration notes and consistency checks.

outputs.description: Complete inventory of legacy point names, telemetry channels, and alarm codes from all three controllers.
The canonical terminology standard listing only new canonical terms for points, telemetry channels, and alarm codes.

subtasks.goal: Write the bilingual integration tutorial and migration notes for Halden.
subtasks.capsule: This subtask handles Part B. It writes a worked integration tutorial with runnable configuration samples in EN/DE, covering HVAC control flow, lighting-scene flow, alarm handling, and migration notes. The migration notes must map every legacy point name and alarm code to its canonical replacement. This subtask depends on root.1's outputs to ensure all terms match the canonical standard exactly.

outputs.description: Bilingual (EN/DE) tutorial section for HVAC control flow with runnable config samples.
Bilingual (EN/DE) tutorial section for lighting-scene flow with runnable config samples.
Bilingual (EN/DE) tutorial section for alarm handling with runnable config samples.
Bilingual (EN/DE) migration notes mapping legacy point names and alarm codes to canonical replacements.

self_role.goal: Integrate both parts into the final bilingual developer style guide and validate term consistency.

outputs.description: The complete, validated, bilingual developer style guide for Halden, combining Part A and Part B with zero term drift.

I will spawn three parallel subtasks to survey each controller (hvac-controller, lighting-bridge, access-gateway) to populate the legacy_survey. subtasks.goal: Survey hvac-controller for legacy point names, telemetry channels, and alarm codes.
subtasks.capsule: Investigate the hvac-controller legacy system. Record all point names (e.g., temperature setpoints), telemetry channels (e.g., airflow rates), and alarm codes (e.g., filter faults). Output a structured list of these legacy terms. This data is required for the global legacy survey.

outputs.description: List of legacy point names, telemetry channels, and alarm codes from hvac-controller.

agent.root_goal: Produce a bilingual (EN/DE) developer style guide for 'Halden', an internal smart-building automation platform, in two independently-owned parts. Part A (device standards team): first survey the naming actually used by Halden's three legacy controllers (hvac-controller, lighting-bridge, access-gateway), recording every legacy point name, telemetry channel, and alarm code in use; then decide and write the canonical terminology standard (point nouns, telemetry-channel vocabulary, alarm-code casing rules). The published standard lists ONLY the new canonical terms, not the legacy ones. Part B (integrator documentation team): write the worked integration tutorial with runnable configuration samples, covering four sections in both languages: the HVAC control flow, the lighting-scene flow, alarm handling, and migration notes for installers upgrading from the legacy controllers - the migration notes must map each legacy point name and alarm code to its canonical replacement, old -> new. Facilities handbook rule, applying to the whole guide: documentation ships only if every point name, telemetry channel, and alarm code appearing anywhere in the guide matches the published canonical standard exactly; term drift between parts is a shipping defect.
